body:
## Issue #468 — "Our system should be able to solve such tasks in agentic mode"

Closes #468.

### The reframing (from the maintainer's comment)

The text-formalization protocol in the issue body — Igor Martynov's *Formal
protocol for translating texts into a knowledge base*, with Pushkin's
**«Сказка о рыбаке и рыбке»** as the canonical input — is **an example of a task**
the system should solve from the prompt. The **core task** is the agentic
capability itself:

> *"the core task is in the comment … we need to make sure our Formal AI is able to
> solve the task in agentic coding mode … call all the tools from any agentic CLI,
> understand errors from tools … do web fetch and web search, to actually complete
> the task."*

Two binding constraints shape the design:

> *"I personally [do] not agree with entities and ontologies (because for us
> everything is a link), while we don't agree with requirements we still should by
> default implement them as is."*

> *"Also don't use claude or codex to connect them to our Formal AI, as that may
> interrupt your own process, and also break execution of other tasks."*

The first means the example task's knowledge base is emitted **directly in Links
Notation** (the `.lino` meta-language already in the code base): the nine
primitives are **realized as links**, not as typed-struct entities/ontologies. The
second means external CLIs (`claude`, `codex`) are pointed **at** the server as
front-ends — they are never wired **into** the engine.

### What ships: the whole agentic loop, offline and deterministic

The new module `src/agentic_coding/` implements both halves of the loop and runs
end-to-end with no network and no neural inference.

1. **The server brain** — a deterministic planner (`plan_chat_step`,
   `src/agentic_coding/planner.rs`): a pure function of the message history that runs
   the state machine `web_search → web_fetch → write_file (formalize) →
   run_command (verify) → final`. `classify_tool` maps whatever tool names a CLI
   advertises onto capabilities (`Search`/`Fetch`/`Write`/`Run`), so the planner
   adapts to *any* subset of tools — *"call all the tools from any agentic CLI"* —
   and `looks_like_error` lets it **react to tool errors** instead of adopting bad
   output.
2. **Three surfaces, one planner** — a shared decision backs
   `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/messages` (what `claude` speaks), and
   `/v1/responses` (what `codex` speaks). The next step is emitted as `tool_calls` /
   a `tool_use` block / a `function_call` item respectively, and a fed-back result
   (`tool` message / `tool_result` / `function_call_output`) is understood in each
   protocol's own idiom so the loop advances. 9 tests in
   `tests/unit/agentic_surfaces.rs` pin the Messages/Responses mirror of the Chat
   behaviour.
3. **Two gates, no hidden autonomy** — tools are refused unless `agent_mode` is
   opted in **and** each tool passes a per-tool permission gate
   (`pkg_agentic_coding`, `src/associative_package.rs`).
4. **The client** — an in-repo driver (`run_agentic_task`,
   `src/agentic_coding/driver.rs`) plays the external CLI: it advertises
   `DRIVER_TOOLS` (`web_search`, `web_fetch`, `write_file`, `run_command`), executes
   each emitted call (search/fetch against an offline corpus; write/run in a
   sandboxed `AgentWorkspace`), feeds results back, **understands tool errors**
   (a 404 fetch is not adopted as a source), and loops until the KB is returned —
   bounded by a hard `MAX_TURNS = 12` cap, so there is no unbounded reasoning loop.
5. **The example task's output** — the formalizer (`formalize_text_to_links`,
   `src/agentic_coding/formalize.rs`) turns the fetched text into a Links Notation
   knowledge base in which **all nine primitives are links** (`PRIMITIVE_KINDS`:
   concept, entity, predicate, assertion, procedure, context, temporal, modal,
   annotation), each record emitted via `format_lino_record`. For the canonical
   synopsis it is **37 records** and `covers_all_nine()` is `true` — both pinned by
   test.

### CLI + worked examples

- `formal-ai agent [task] [--transcript]` drives the full
  `search → fetch → write → run → final` loop offline; `--transcript` prints every
  tool call and result before the final answer.
- `examples/issue_468_agentic_loop.rs` — the loop end-to-end.
- `examples/issue_468_formalize_text.rs` — the formalizer tour over the nine
  primitives.

### Case study + requirements (R306–R319)

`docs/case-studies/issue-468/`:

- **README.md** — collected data, deep analysis, online research, the exhaustive
  requirement list **R306–R319**, per-requirement solution plans, a prior-art
  survey (agentic front-ends, benchmarks, AMR / OpenIE / RDF reification & RDF-star /
  named graphs / Wikidata qualifiers / FrameNet & PropBank / the associative model),
  the agentic-mode section, and a risks table.
- **formal-protocol-mapping.md** — each of the nine primitives shown as a Links
  Notation record (discharges **R311**).
- **raw-data/** — the issue/PR captures plus a summarized-and-cited source-protocol
  summary and the online-research notes.

`REQUIREMENTS.md` carries rows **R306–R319** (R314, agentic-coding mode, is the core
task and is **implemented**); the meta-algorithm is also captured as a grounded
`data/meta/agentic-coding-recipe.lino` recipe.
`docs/desktop/server-api.md` §4e documents the multi-surface loop for operators.

### Honestly out of scope (not hidden)

- **General open-domain NL→KB extraction** (the article's §7 learned pipeline) needs
  neural inference — a project NON-GOAL. The formalizer annotates *every* sentence
  but emits a *structured* assertion only when a closed-class lexicon recognises the
  triple; unrecognised sentences become honest natural-language assertions that
  **never guess**.
- **Live network access** in CI — the driver resolves web tools against a fixed
  offline corpus, so the loop is reproducible.

### Verification (commit `6ea2835a`)

- **963 unit tests** green (`cargo test --test unit`) — incl. `agentic_coding.rs`,
  `agentic_surfaces.rs` (9), `specification/agentic_meta_algorithm.rs` grounding,
  and `docs_requirements_issue_468.rs` (pins all four docs to the live code).
- **419 integration + source tests** green; semantic-grounding step green (5/5).
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — **zero warnings**, MSRV 1.70-clean.
- `cargo fmt --check` clean; `cargo test --doc` clean; the file-size guard passes;
  changelog-fragment and version-modification guards pass.
title: feat(issue-468): solve tasks in agentic-coding mode — server-driven tool loop across three OpenAI surfaces

body:
Fixes https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/368

## Summary
- Added a dedicated README section for connecting Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and Link Assistant Agent to `formal-ai serve`.
- Corrected the server API guide so Codex uses the Responses wire API and OpenCode/Agent use OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions provider config.
- Added docs traceability coverage and a live loopback integration test that exercises `/v1/models`, `/v1/responses`, `/v1/chat/completions`, and `/v1/messages` with bearer auth.

## Reproduction
Before this PR, the README had no dedicated agentic AI tools section, and the server API guide still documented Codex with `wire_api = "chat"` plus an unsupported Agent `OPENAI_BASE_URL` flow.

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --test unit issue_368_readme_documents_agentic_cli_setup -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test --test integration cli_serve_exposes_agentic_tool_protocols_over_loopback -- --nocapture`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- `GITHUB_EVENT_NAME=pull_request GITHUB_HEAD_REF=issue-368-485e22098964 GITHUB_BASE_REF=main rust-script scripts/check-version-modification.rs`
- `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- `bun run build:web`
- `git diff --exit-code -- src/web/vendor.bundle.js src/web/ocr.bundle.js`
- `npm ci --prefix tests/e2e`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:i18n`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-parity`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-test-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:intent-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:web-tdz`
- `npm run vscode:test`

No screenshots: documentation and protocol integration changes only.
title: Document agentic CLI server setup

body:
## Summary

Implements **E4** of the #511 desktop agent epic for issue #516.

- Adds a desktop `AgentProvider` seam with a default hermetic `in-process` provider and an opt-in `commander` provider selected by `FORMAL_AI_AGENT_PROVIDER=commander`.
- The in-process provider keeps read-only terminal execution on the existing desktop tool gate / Docker sandbox and can drive the existing `formal-ai agent --task ...` Rust agentic loop for non-command tasks.
- The commander provider invokes `agent-commander` via `start-agent --tool agent`, points it at the local OpenAI-compatible Formal AI server, maps desktop grants to coarse `--plan-only` / `--read-only` / `--approve-each` restrictions, and scrubs host subscription credentials.
- Adds a recursive desktop CI/static guard that fails if direct host `agent`, `claude`, or `codex` spawns are introduced.

## Reproduction / coverage

Before this PR there was no provider abstraction, no agent-commander path, and no static guard preventing direct host agent CLI spawns.

Added `desktop/scripts/agent-provider.test.mjs` covering:

- default provider is `in-process` and hermetic,
- `ls ~` executes through the in-process provider via the permission-gated shell router,
- in-process tasks drive the local `formal-ai agent` loop without host API tokens,
- commander launches `start-agent --tool agent` with local server env and no host subscription env,
- desktop grants map to plan-only/read-only/approve-each restrictions,
- desktop JS sources do not directly spawn host `agent`/`claude`/`codex` binaries.

## Verification

- `npm --prefix desktop test` — 48 tests + smoke passed.
- `node --check desktop/lib/agent-provider.cjs && node --check desktop/main.cjs && node --check desktop/preload.cjs` — passed.
- `cargo fmt --check` — passed.
- `cargo test` — passed: 1016 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored.
- `git diff --check` — passed.

Note: `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` could not run in this environment because `rust-script` is not installed; touched files are below the 1000-line guard (`agent-provider.cjs` 598 lines, test 224 lines).

Closes #516.
title: E4: AgentProvider seam for desktop agent execution

body:
## Summary

Fixes #479. Desktop apps showed **"Not available in latest release"** on `/download` for every platform. Root cause: the desktop-release workflow resolved the **parent** commit SHA, but the auto-release tag is created on the **child** `chore: release` commit, so the exact-SHA match never succeeded and **v0.187.0–v0.201.0 shipped zero desktop assets**. This PR fixes that, refreshes the obsolete screenshots, adds the macOS install screenshots, restructures the site into a landing → app / docs / download chooser with CI/CD for every surface (including a generated `/docs/api` Rust reference), and records a full case study.

## What changed

### 1. Desktop release availability (the bug)
- New `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh` with **two-tier resolution**: tier 1 exact-SHA match, tier 2 "latest published release / auto-release child commit". This is the fix that makes desktop assets attach to the right release. Verbose logging added for future diagnosis.
- Guarded by `tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs` (8 tests covering exact-SHA, child-commit, idempotency, dispatch, and skip paths).
- Verified across the whole codebase: the only `workflow_run`/`head_sha` consumer is `desktop-release.yml`, and it now flows through the fixed script — no other instance of the bug shape.

### 2. Obsolete + missing screenshots
- Regenerated the `/download` desktop app-preview and page screenshots.
- Added macOS Gatekeeper install screenshots (mirroring vk-bot-desktop), covered by `tests/e2e/tests/issue-479.spec.js`.

### 3. Site restructure with CI/CD for every surface
```
/            landing-page chooser   (src/web/index.html + landing.{js,css})
/app/        the interactive web app (relocated; <base href="../">)
/docs/       documentation hub       (src/web/docs/ → /docs/api + prose docs)
/docs/api/   Rust API reference       (cargo doc, generated at deploy time)
/download/   desktop download page
```
- The app moves under `/app/` with `<base href="../">` so all relative asset/worker/seed URLs still resolve to the shared root — no asset edits, correct under both Pages' `/formal-ai/` prefix and the desktop static server. The desktop wrapper now opens `/app/index.html`.
- Landing + docs render through a shared `src/web/site-chrome.js` chooser factory: shared Links-Notation preference store (theme + UI language carry across pages), strict same-origin CSP, `<noscript>` fallback, localized into en/ru/zh/hi.
- `release.yml`'s `deploy-demo` job generates the API reference with `cargo doc --no-deps --lib` (+ a root redirect so `/docs/api/` doesn't 404) and copies it into the Pages artifact **after** stamping. Job timeout 15→20m for the cold-cache compile; concurrency made **main-safe** so a release run is never cancelled mid-flight (matching `desktop-release.yml`).
- New `tests/e2e/tests/issue-479-site.spec.js` exercises both chooser pages (nav targets, app boot under base href, language/theme persistence, hidden version label on un-stamped builds), deployment-agnostic so it runs on local **and** Pages configs. `workflow_release.rs` gains guards for the new static + CI invariants.

### 4. CI hygiene (unblocks the Lint job)
- Newer stable Rust promoted two clippy lints to deny-by-default, which were silently failing `cargo clippy --all-targets` under `-D warnings` (masked behind the first error):
  - `src/agentic_coding/lexicon.rs`: `(len + 1) / 2` → `len.div_ceil(2)` (`manual_div_ceil`; `div_ceil` is stable since 1.73, within the crate's 1.77 MSRV — the avoidance comment cited a stale 1.70).
  - `tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs`: backtick `GITHUB_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_OUTPUT` in docs (`doc_markdown`) and `#[allow(clippy::literal_string_with_formatting_args)]` on the mock-gh raw shell string.
- Added the changelog fragment for #479 (`bump: minor`).

### 5. Case study & template comparison
- `docs/case-studies/issue-479/`: timeline reconstruction, full requirement list, root-cause analysis, candidate solutions, surveyed components — plus the downloaded raw logs/data and a side-by-side comparison of the four link-foundation CI/CD templates.
- **No #479-analogous bug exists in any template** (none drive desktop releases via `workflow_run`/`head_sha`); `template-comparison/REPORT.md` enumerates the cross-cutting best practices worth porting upstream. Since the mandatory "report the same bug" trigger is not met and filing on external repos is outward-facing, those enhancements are **documented (not filed)** and ready to open on request.

## Testing — CI green ✅
All PR checks pass: **Lint and Format**, **Test (ubuntu-latest)**, **E2E Tests (local demo)**, **Code Coverage**, **Build Package**, **Changelog Fragment**, **Detect Changes**, **Version Modification**. (Deploy/release jobs are intentionally `main`-only and skip on PRs.)

Locally:
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd` → **61 passed** (8 desktop-resolve + 2 new restructure guards + existing guards updated for the new layout). Clippy & rustfmt clean.
- Full local Playwright suite → **285 passed**; `cargo doc` verified (emits `target/doc/formal_ai/index.html`). The only local failures are the 5 `issue-221` *live-Wiktionary* tests, which fail **only** in the network-isolated sandbox (HTTP 403 to wiktionary.org) — untouched by this PR and **green in CI's E2E job**.

## Verbatim requirement coverage
✅ Desktop apps available again (root-cause fix) · ✅ refreshed screenshots · ✅ macOS install screenshots · ✅ vk-bot-desktop best practices · ✅ CI/CD for `/`, `/app`, `/docs/*`, `/docs/api`, `/download` · ✅ landing chooser to app/docs/download · ✅ CI/CD template comparison (no analogous bug; enhancements documented) · ✅ case study with downloaded logs/data · ✅ verbose/debug output added · ✅ applied across the whole codebase · ✅ single PR.
title: fix(desktop-release): restore desktop downloads + restructure site into landing/app/docs/download (#479)

body:
Closes #438

## Summary

### Prepared Telegram Docker image (original issue #438)
- Publish the Docker-in-Docker Telegram bot image to GHCR on release as `ghcr.io/link-assistant/formal-ai:latest` plus the release version tag, without requiring Docker Hub secrets.
- Add root `compose.yaml` and README/architecture docs for `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123:abc docker compose up` and one-line `docker run` startup.
- Preserve optional Docker Hub mirroring/local builds, add GHCR release-note badges, repository source metadata, requirements rows, changelog, and the issue-438 case study under `docs/case-studies/issue-438/`.

### One-click / one-line services (follow-up maintainer request, 2026-06-17)
- **Desktop one-click:** a new **Services** panel in the desktop sidebar starts and stops *both* prepared containers — the Telegram bot (`formal-ai-telegram`) and the OpenAI-compatible API server (`formal-ai-server`, `formal-ai serve` on `127.0.0.1:8080`) — with one click, polling Docker for live status. The bot requires `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` before it starts.
- **Testable backend:** lifecycle logic lives in `desktop/lib/service-control.cjs` (dependency-injected `runDocker`, `docker inspect -f '{{.State.Running}}'` status, stale-container reaping, required-token check), exposed over IPC by `desktop/main.cjs` (`formalAiDesktop:serviceStatus`/`startService`/`stopService`), bridged through the hardened `desktop/preload.cjs`, and rendered in `src/web/app.js`.
- **Server one-line:** `compose.yaml` gains an opt-in `server` profile so a server reproduces the identical containers with one line (`docker compose --profile all up -d`), while `docker compose up` keeps starting only the Telegram bot. Each Docker-in-Docker service gets its own inner-Docker volume (`formal-ai-telegram-docker`, `formal-ai-server-docker`) — two DinD daemons cannot share one `/var/lib/docker`. Neither path bind-mounts the host `docker.sock`; each uses its own inner daemon.
- **Documentation:** new `docs/desktop/service-control.md` documents both paths in detail (service table, desktop wiring, compose profiles, raw `docker run`, configuration, rationale), with README "One-click services" and ARCHITECTURE "One-click / one-line services" sections and REQUIREMENTS rows R327–R329.
- Latest `main` merged into the branch.

## Reproduction
- Before the fix, `cargo test --test unit docker_runtime -- --nocapture` failed because `compose.yaml` was missing.
- Before the fix, `cargo test --test unit release_workflow_publishes_prebuilt_ghcr_image_after_crate_is_visible_and_optional_docker_hub_mirror -- --nocapture` failed because release publishing did not include GHCR.
- Before the fix, `cargo test --test unit issue_438_prebuilt_telegram_image_documents_are_present_and_traceable -- --nocapture` failed because issue-438 traceability docs and requirements were absent.
- The raw before/after logs are committed under `docs/case-studies/issue-438/raw-data/`.

## Verification
- `cargo test --test unit` — 1009 passed, 0 failed
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — clean (0 warnings)
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `desktop` `npm test` (30) + `node scripts/service-control.test.mjs` (16) — all pass
- `docker compose config` — valid (telegram default + `--profile server`/`--profile all`)
- New service-control tests: `tests/unit/specification/desktop_surface.rs::{desktop_service_control_starts_and_stops_prepared_containers,desktop_web_surface_exposes_one_click_service_controls}`, `tests/unit/docker_runtime.rs::compose_file_offers_optional_openai_compatible_server_profile`

## Notes
- The desktop one-click and the server one-line paths deliberately drive the **same** container arguments, so behaviour is identical everywhere.
- Online research sources are captured in `docs/case-studies/issue-438/raw-data/online-research.md`.
title: Prepare Telegram Docker image + one-click/one-line Telegram bot and OpenAI server

body:
## Issue #511 — case study, requirements, and full agent-mode implementation

Fixes #511.

### What the issue is
A Russian desktop prompt asking to run `` `ls ~` `` in the terminal fell through to the worker's `unknown` fallback. The maintainer ask is larger than that visible bug: surface chat / agent / full-auto mode from the start, request per-command permissions, execute through `link-assistant/agent` via `agent-commander`, isolate execution in a Formal-AI container, and cover the cold-start journey with integration/e2e tests.

### What this PR delivers
The work was decomposed into eight verifiable milestones (E1–E8, tracked as #513–#520) and **every milestone is now merged into this branch**:

- **E1 (#513 / PR #525)** — terminal-command intent in the Rust engine and JS worker (seed-backed, total-closure grounded, worker-mirror CI guard) + the three-way **chat / agent / full-auto** mode radio. Terminal prompts no longer dead-end in `unknown`.
- **E2 (#514 / PR #528)** — agent onboarding system message and the desktop per-tool permission panel (grant/decline each tool), persisted in preferences, fully internationalized (en/ru/zh/hi).
- **E3 (#515 / PR #530)** — agent-mode local OpenAI-compatible server auto-start (`ensureAgentServer`) wired to the desktop status surface.
- **E4 (#516 / PR #532)** — the `AgentProvider` seam: an in-process default (hermetic/offline/deterministic) plus an opt-in `agent-commander` provider, with the command-approval flow and default-deny tool router as the enforcement point.
- **E5 (#517 / PR #533)** — the installable `formal-ai-agent` container (Dockerfile + `compose.yaml` agent profile) bundling `link-assistant/agent` + `agent-commander`, with one-click install/health-check from the desktop services panel.
- **E6 (#518 / PR #536)** — NDJSON agent-output rendering mapped onto the existing chat message / tool-call render path.
- **E7 (#519 / PR #537)** — the cold-start `ls ~` e2e journey (hermetic in-process variant always-on; container-gated commander variant opt-in via `FORMAL_AI_E2E_AGENT_COMMANDER=1`).
- **E8 (#520 / PR #539)** — upstream re-verification (`agent` v0.24.0, `agent-commander` js_0.8.0 / rust_0.2.6, no open gaps) and the finalized Agent CLI + agent-commander best-practices write-up.

`docs/case-studies/issue-511/` carries the full case study, the R1–R21 requirements inventory with per-requirement evidence, the solution plans, and the (now-completed) E1–E8 epic. Raw PR/issue snapshots live under `raw-data/`.

### Upstream feedback (R16)
- `agent#271` (no native permission system) → **resolved** by `agent#272` (v0.24.0).
- `agent-commander#39` (map `--read-only` for the `agent` tool) → **closed** (js_0.7.0 / rust_0.2.5).
- `agent-commander#40` (uniform `--approve-each` per-command relay) → **closed** (js_0.8.0 / rust_0.2.6).

No open `agent-commander` gaps remain. The one residual limitation — approve-each relays only for `agent`/`claude` because `codex`/`gemini`/`qwen` expose no headless approval handshake — is a documented upstream-CLI constraint.

### Constraints honored
- **Isolation:** autonomous execution runs only inside the Formal-AI container, never against the host's `claude`/`codex`. The in-process provider keeps CI hermetic; the container/commander path is opt-in.
- **Indirection:** the desktop app drives `agent-commander`, which drives `agent` — never the Agent CLI directly.
- **Default-deny:** the per-tool gate (`tool-router.cjs` `isPermitted`) remains the enforcement point; the UI only records grants.
- **No hardcoded natural language:** all new user-facing prose is seed-backed (engines) or routed through the i18n catalog (web), enforced by CI guards.

### Tests run locally
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `npm ci --prefix tests/e2e`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:i18n`
- `GITHUB_BASE_REF=main npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-parity`
- `GITHUB_BASE_REF=main npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-test-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:intent-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:web-tdz`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:web-hardcoded-ui`
- `node experiments/issue-513-sync-worker-terminal.mjs --check`
- `node --test desktop/scripts/service-control.test.mjs`
- `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- `bun run build:web`
- `git diff --exit-code -- src/web/vendor.bundle.js src/web/ocr.bundle.js`
- `npm run vscode:test`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e test:local -- tests/issue-511-cold-start.spec.js`
title: Issue #511: agent-mode case study, requirements, and full implementation (E1–E8)
